what_went_right: Per the feature-bundling-causes-styling-iteration-tail memory, the work was correctly split into 5 separate feature commits (whitespace, Concise/Elaborate, kebab menu, Tune AI Model, Tune Prompt) plus 2 doc/fix commits — no single-bundle smash like v0.1.13 or v0.1.20. The split itself did NOT generate a styling iteration tail; each feature shipped clean with no follow-on cosmetic fix commit.

Pre-tag review gate fired and was load-bearing AGAIN (third confirmed instance): code-reviewer pass #1 caught CHANGELOG cost figures off by 2-10x (commit 2890b76 corrected); code-reviewer pass #2 caught README cost table off by 20-30% across every row + the Reset dialog missing model/budget bullet (commit 2f7c550 corrected). Both fixed inline before the push, exactly as the pre-tag-review-gate-is-load-bearing memory prescribes.

Test suite was real: 186 tests green after the bundle, including new coverage for buildPreviewUserMessage variants, buildSystemPrompt overrides, MODEL_CATALOG structure, and model override wiring across all three providers (test/ai-providers.test.mjs +95 lines, test/ai-context.test.mjs +50 lines). Where the work-agent broke a contract (lens to lensHint), the existing test failure forced an explicit refactor of the test inside the same commit instead of silent skew.

Session-start memory consultation happened and was visible: the assistant emitted the 'Memories I'm operating with' block at 19:17:38 citing parallelize-from-the-second-file. This is the protocol working — even when the rule didn't fire in practice (see what_went_wrong), the consultation step succeeded.
what_went_wrong: TaskCreate was adopted at 20:13:30 — AFTER the user's third request (kebab menu) — and ONLY after the work-agent had already shipped commits 8bdcdf1 (60K bump) and d6fb834 (Concise/Elaborate). The 'compact whitespace + Concise/Elaborate buttons' request at 20:04 was TWO independent items and should have tripped the gate; it did not. The parallelize-from-the-second-file memory was loaded but not applied. Loud signal: ToolSearch had to be called to fetch TaskCreate's schema mid-task, proving the work-agent hadn't pre-loaded it.

Even once TaskCreate was adopted, the 5 calls were sequential (back-to-back from 20:13:35 to 20:13:57) and the BACKGROUND sub-agent fan-out from the Multi-file Build Protocol was skipped entirely. Only 3 Agent invocations across the whole session (47 Bash + 39 Edit + 21 Read): two code-reviewer runs (one per phase) and one retro. No per-file background code-reviewer, no per-file test-writing general-purpose agent. The protocol was nominally honored (tasks created) but the execution mechanism (BACKGROUND fan-out) was not used.

Same arithmetic bug landed TWICE: CHANGELOG cost figures off by ~2x and 10x in commit 8bdcdf1 (fixed by 2890b76), then README cost table off by 20-30% across every row in commit 499ecf4 (fixed by 2f7c550). Both were hand-rolled estimates by the work-agent. The codebase shipped estimateCostUsd() and MODEL_CATALOG in the same session — the doc figures should have been derived from that function (or at minimum cross-checked against it). Code-reviewer's nit explicitly called out the missing test for estimateCostUsd, which would have closed this loop.

Single end-of-bundle code-reviewer pass at 20:31:46 against 5 commits caught both contract drift (lens to lensHint refactor in commit 0f17ebb broke an existing test) and arithmetic drift (README cost table). The lens refactor was salvaged because the work-agent ran the test suite inside the same commit and noticed the failure (transcript 20:24:52: 'I need to update the existing lens-override test since I removed the lens: neutral keyword handling'). But that's a lucky catch driven by test coverage existing for that path — a less-covered signature change would have shipped broken and only been caught at end-of-bundle review.
three_whys: Why 1 (surface problem): The work-agent caught two cost-figure arithmetic bugs only at end-of-bundle code-reviewer pass, not during the commits that introduced them — so each had to be fixed in a follow-on commit (2890b76 and 2f7c550) rather than landing right the first time.

Why 2 (underlying cause): The Multi-file Build Protocol's BACKGROUND fan-out (per-file code-reviewer + per-file test-writer sub-agents as each file lands) was not used. Five sequential TaskCreate calls without any concurrent Agent invocation against the in-flight commits meant nothing was reviewing the cost figures or the lens contract while the next commit was being written.

Why 3 (root cause): The parallelize-from-the-second-file memory was consulted at session start (visible in the Memories block at 19:17:38) but the rule does not fire automatically — it requires the work-agent to ask the gate question on EVERY new user request. In this session it didn't fire on the user's first multi-item request ('compact whitespace + Concise/Elaborate buttons', 2 items, 2+ files) and only fired retroactively when the kebab menu request made the multi-step nature impossible to ignore. Loading a memory at session start is a stale ambient signal; the acute trigger is the user's next message, and that trigger was missed twice.

action_items.title: Add a unit test for estimateCostUsd in test/ai-providers.test.mjs
action_items.description: Pure function with no DOM or storage deps. Test inputs: 60K chars + each MODEL_CATALOG entry. Assert input tokens (15000), output tokens (800), totalUsd within +/-0.1% of the hand-computed expected value per model. This closes the loop on the static-doc-cost-figures-compute-dont-estimate learning — the moment pricing drifts in MODEL_CATALOG, the test breaks, and a doc-regen script (next item) can rebuild the README + CHANGELOG cost tables from the function's output.

action_items.title: Add npm run docs:cost-table that regenerates README + CHANGELOG cost tables from estimateCostUsd + MODEL_CATALOG, with a CI assert that committed tables match
action_items.description: Replace the hand-rolled cost tables in README.md (under 'AI Insights' / 'Tune AI model') and any future CHANGELOG entries with a generated block (e.g., bracketed by <!-- generated:cost-table --> markers). Script reads MODEL_CATALOG, calls estimateCostUsd at the 60K default for each entry, formats as markdown table, writes in place. CI step (or pre-commit hook) re-runs the generator and fails if the working tree differs from the regenerated output. This is the structural fix for both cost-table strikes in this session and for the next pricing change in MODEL_CATALOG.

action_items.title: Add a global rule: on every new user request, the assistant must visibly ask the gate question (>=2 files OR >=2 independent items?) before the first tool call
action_items.description: Goes in ~/.claude/tech-debt.md. The parallelize-from-the-second-file memory captures the rule but agents are loading it at session start and then failing to apply it on the second/third user request of the session (this session is the second confirmed instance — the precursor memory's own session was the first). The fix is a forcing function: every new user request opens with a one-line visible decision ('Gate: 2+ files/items? yes/no -> next call is TaskCreate / Edit'). This is the same pattern as the 'Memories I'm operating with' block from CLAUDE.md — a loud, visible action the user can challenge. Worth promoting from memory to a top-level CLAUDE.md rule under HIGH PRIORITY: Parallelism-First Execution. Belongs in tech-debt.md because it's a meta-improvement that applies across every project, not just BetterSSC.

action_items.title: When a commit touches a lib/ export's signature, that same commit must (a) run the test suite and (b) spawn a background code-reviewer on its diff
action_items.description: Goes in ~/.claude/tech-debt.md. The Multi-file Build Protocol already says BACKGROUND review per file, but the protocol's enforcement is missing — this session ran code-reviewer ONCE at end-of-bundle despite touching three lib/ signatures (buildPreviewUserMessage, buildSystemPrompt, and the three provider buildRequest functions in lib/ai-providers.js). Tighten the protocol by adding a concrete trigger: any diff that modifies an `export function` / `export const` shape in a lib/ file invokes a background code-reviewer agent at commit time. The lens -> lensHint refactor would have been flagged inside commit 0f17ebb instead of at end-of-bundle review.
